    Home › Compare › Airbus A380 vs Boeing 747

    Airbus A380 vs Boeing 747

    Both aircraft at true relative scale — against a 1.8 m person and a double-decker bus.

    Wingspan and overall height are drawn to true scale from the sourced figures on each aircraft page. Compare any other aircraft in the live tool →

    The numbers

    Side by side

    Airbus A380Boeing 747DifferenceBigger
    Length72.72 m76.25 m3.53 mBoeing 747 +5%
    Wingspan79.75 m68.40 m11.35 mAirbus A380 +17%
    Height24.09 m19.40 m4.69 mAirbus A380 +24%
    MTOW575 t448 t127 tAirbus A380 +28%
    Typical seats525410115Airbus A380 +28%
    Range14,800 km14,320 km480 kmAirbus A380 +3%
    Max speed945 km/h988 km/h43 km/hBoeing 747 +5%
    The verdict

    Which is actually bigger?

    The 747 invented the widebody and ruled for thirty-eight years; the A380 was built to dethrone it and arrived exactly as the market it was designed for evaporated. The A380 wins every dimension that can be measured — longer wings, taller, heavier, hundreds more seats — and lost the argument anyway. Point-to-point flying dissolved the hub-and-spoke logic both aircraft were built on, and the twin-engine 777 and 787 finished the pair of them off.

    The Boeing 747 is the longer of the two, at 76.25 m against 72.72 m — a difference of 3.53 m. Wingspan tells the opposite story: the Airbus A380 spreads 11.35 m wider, so which one is "bigger" depends entirely on which dimension you care about.

    The largest passenger aircraft ever built. The original jumbo jet. The silhouettes above are drawn at true relative scale from the sourced figures on each aircraft's page — same metres, same pixels, no artistic licence.

    On capacity, the Airbus A380 carries more people in a typical layout — 525 against 410.

    Airbus A380

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
